mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) or negative mean during rate cut periods over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.5 percent, labor market slack signals weakening consumer spending and corporate earnings deterioration, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.5%, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand recovery, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns, with out-of-sample accuracy below 52% over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the index of industrial production during late-cycle expansions signals supply-side bottlenecks and impending economic slowdown, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during late-cycle phases do not precede negative 5-day returns, or the signal's predictive power vanishes when regime filters are applied.

mechanism_narrative: Lower month-over-month inflation readings reduce near-term expectations for aggressive monetary tightening, supporting equity risk premiums and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL declines consistently coincide with negative 5-day forward returns, or the correlation coefficient remains below 0.05 across all rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth falling below 1.0 percent indicates severe demand contraction that forces earnings downgrades, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in >65% of quarters where GDPC1 growth drops below 1.0%, or the signal shows zero predictive IC over a 3-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, boosting corporate revenue expectations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS/UNRATE signal fails to separate positive from negative 60-day return periods, with classification accuracy below 50% in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during post-shock recovery phases indicates productive capacity expansion without price pressures, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during recovery regimes do not precede positive 20-day returns, or the mechanism's Sharpe ratio remains below 0.5 over a 4-year walk-forward evaluation.
